tasks.criterion: `pyproject.toml` is uv-installable and collects tests: running `uv sync` exits 0, and `uv run pytest --collect-only` exits 0 and reports at least 10 test items collected from `tests/`.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv sync && uv run pytest --collect-only -q 2>&1 | grep -E "^[0-9]+ tests? collected" | head -1'

tasks.criterion: Core Pydantic models are importable and validate correctly: Sample, Score, Task, EvalLog all import; Sample round-trips model_dump(); target field is optional.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/core/test_models.py -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/c2.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/c2.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: Decorator registry round-trip — all five decorator types (@task, @solver, @scorer, @metric, @tool) register the decorated function by __name__ and the stored callable executes correctly.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/core/test_decorators.py -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/c3.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/c3.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: `AnthropicModel` defaults to model='claude-opus-4-7' and effort='medium'; accepts effort='high'; rejects invalid effort values like 'turbo'.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/models/test_anthropic.py -k "defaults or effort" -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/c4.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/c4.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: Thinking-block round-trip test exits 0 and emits the literal phrase 'thinking blocks preserved byte-identical' (F6, deterministic). The test uses mocked SDK responses and asserts the thinking block fields survive the round-trip.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/models/test_anthropic.py -k thinking_round_trip -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/c5a.txt && grep -q "thinking blocks preserved byte-identical" /tmp/c5a.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/c5a.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: Thinking-block round-trip assertion structure is correct (F6, llm-judge): the test_thinking_block_round_trip function mocks anthropic.Anthropic, simulates a two-turn exchange, and asserts that the thinking block's signature field (and type/thinking fields) survive byte-identical in the second messages.create call's messages argument. A test that only checks call-count is FAIL.
`uv run pytest` green across `tests/core/` and `tests/models/`: exits 0 with zero failures and zero errors.
`trine-eval --help` exits 0 and stdout contains the strings 'run', 'score', and 'report'.
tasks.verification_command: uv run trine-eval --help 2>&1 | tee /tmp/help.txt && grep -q 'run' /tmp/help.txt && grep -q 'score' /tmp/help.txt && grep -q 'report' /tmp/help.txt && echo PASS

tasks.criterion: Phase 2 contract naming convention documented in `rules/harness-conventions.md`: the file contains the string 'contracts/phase-02' in a new subsection covering the canonical paths for Phase 2 contract files.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'contracts/phase-02' rules/harness-conventions.md && echo PASS || echo FAIL

tasks.criterion: `AnthropicModel` correctly wires the interleaved-thinking beta header ('interleaved-thinking-2025-05-14') and maps effort tier to budget_tokens (low<=1000, medium<=8000, high<=16000, xhigh<=32000, max<=100000); thinking blocks are never stripped or mutated on round-trip.

`pyproject.toml` metadata quality and dependency completeness: name='trine-eval', version='0.1.0', requires-python='>=3.12', pydantic>=2.0 and anthropic>=0.40 in runtime deps, pytest/pytest-asyncio/ruff/mypy in dev deps, [project.scripts] defines trine-eval entry point, no forbidden packages (langgraph/ragas/pgvector/fastapi).
`uv.lock` exists at repo root and is tracked by git: `[ -f uv.lock ] && git ls-files uv.lock | grep -q uv.lock` succeeds. This enforces that `uv sync` was run and the lock file was committed.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c '[ -f uv.lock ] && git ls-files uv.lock | grep -q uv.lock && echo PASS || echo FAIL'

tasks.criterion: Historical Phase 1 contracts and evals are unmodified: no file under .harness/contracts/sprint-0[1-9]*.md, .harness/contracts/sprint-1*.md, .harness/evals/sprint-0[1-9]*.md, or .harness/evals/sprint-1*.md is modified on the Sprint 01 branch (checked against merge-base with main).
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'BASE=$(git merge-base HEAD main 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse HEAD~$(git rev-list --count HEAD)^); diff_out=$(git diff $BASE..HEAD -- ".harness/contracts/sprint-0[1-9]*.md" ".harness/contracts/sprint-1*.md" ".harness/evals/sprint-0[1-9]*.md" ".harness/evals/sprint-1*.md" 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$diff_out" ] && echo PASS || (echo FAIL && echo "$diff_out" && exit 1)'

tasks.criterion: No forbidden packages (langgraph, ragas, pgvector, fastapi) appear in pyproject.toml, src/trine_eval/, or tests/.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'result=$(git grep -rn -E "langgraph|ragas|pgvector|fastapi" pyproject.toml src/ tests/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^Binary" | grep -v ":0$"); [ -z "$result" ] && echo PASS || (echo FAIL && echo "$result" && exit 1)'
